questions.q: In which Indian state is the Dwarkadhish Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Dwarkadhish Temple stands in the coastal town of Dwarka in the state of Gujarat.

questions.q: To which Hindu deity is the Dwarkadhish Temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: The temple is dedicated to Krishna, worshipped here as Dwarkadhish, the 'King of Dwarka'.

questions.q: What does the name 'Dwarkadhish' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Dwarkadhish' translates to 'King of Dwarka', a title of Krishna who is said to have ruled the city.

questions.q: The Dwarkadhish Temple is one of which group of four sacred pilgrimage sites?
questions.explanation: Dwarka is one of the Char Dham, the four principal Hindu pilgrimage destinations established across India.

questions.q: A distinctive daily ritual at the Dwarkadhish Temple involves what object?
questions.explanation: A great flag atop the temple is changed multiple times each day in a long-standing ritual.

questions.q: How many storeys does the main Dwarkadhish Temple structure have?
questions.explanation: The main shrine is a towering five-storey structure supported by numerous pillars.

questions.q: The Dwarkadhish Temple sits near the confluence of a river and which body of water?
questions.explanation: The temple overlooks the Gomti river's meeting with the Arabian Sea on Gujarat's western coast.

questions.q: Which river flows beside the Dwarkadhish Temple at Dwarka?
questions.explanation: The Gomti river of Dwarka flows beside the temple before reaching the sea, and pilgrims bathe at its ghats.

questions.q: What is the emblem most associated with Krishna and the Dwarkadhish Temple?
questions.explanation: The conch (shankha) is a classic emblem of Vishnu and Krishna, the deity worshipped at Dwarka.

questions.q: Dwarka is traditionally regarded as the kingdom that Krishna established after leaving which city?
questions.explanation: Hindu tradition holds that Krishna left Mathura and founded the coastal kingdom of Dwarka.

questions.q: By what other name is the main Dwarkadhish Temple commonly known?
questions.explanation: The temple is also called Jagat Mandir, meaning 'temple of the universe'.

questions.q: Hindu tradition credits the original Dwarka temple to which descendant of Krishna?
questions.explanation: Legend holds the original shrine over Krishna's residence was built by his great-grandson Vajranabha.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the main spire (shikhara) of the Dwarkadhish Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple's towering shikhara rises to roughly 78 metres (about 235 feet) above the ground.

questions.q: The two main gateways of the Dwarkadhish Temple are traditionally known as the Swarga Dwar and which other?
questions.explanation: Pilgrims enter through the Swarga Dwar (gate of heaven) and exit through the Moksha Dwar (gate of liberation).

questions.q: A long flight of steps leads from the Dwarkadhish Temple's Swarga Dwar down to which feature?
questions.explanation: Stone steps descend from the heaven-gate down to the ghats of the Gomti river where pilgrims bathe.

questions.q: In which broad architectural style is the Dwarkadhish Temple built?
questions.explanation: The temple is built in the North-Indian Nagara/Chalukyan tradition, with a tall curvilinear shikhara over the sanctum.

questions.q: Roughly how many carved pillars support the multi-storey main hall of the Dwarkadhish Temple?
questions.explanation: The five-storey structure is traditionally said to be supported by around 72 intricately carved pillars.

questions.q: The Dwarkadhish Temple is most often associated with which medieval reformer who is said to have visited and worshipped there?
questions.explanation: Adi Shankaracharya is traditionally linked with Dwarka, where he is said to have established one of his four maths (Sharada Peeth).

questions.q: Which of the four cardinal maths founded by Adi Shankaracharya is located at Dwarka?
questions.explanation: Dwarka hosts the western math, the Sharada Peeth (also called Kalika Math), one of Shankaracharya's four seats.

questions.q: Pilgrims traditionally combine a visit to the Dwarkadhish Temple with the nearby island shrine of which form of Krishna?
questions.explanation: Bet Dwarka, an island near Dwarka, is revered as Krishna's residence and is a customary part of the pilgrimage.

questions.q: The flag flown atop the Dwarkadhish Temple is notable for typically bearing images of which two celestial symbols?
questions.explanation: The temple's dhwaja (flag) characteristically depicts the sun and the moon, associated with Krishna's enduring presence.

questions.q: Which festival is celebrated with the greatest fervour at the Dwarkadhish Temple?
questions.explanation: Janmashtami, marking Krishna's birth, is the temple's most important and elaborately observed festival.

questions.q: The present main structure of the Dwarkadhish Temple is generally dated by historians to around which period?
options: The 15th-16th century CE, on much older foundations
questions.explanation: The current temple largely dates to about the 15th-16th century CE, rebuilt and expanded over far older foundations.

questions.q: Underwater archaeological exploration off the Dwarka coast was led by which Indian marine archaeologist?
questions.explanation: Marine archaeologist S. R. Rao led the National Institute of Oceanography's underwater excavations off Dwarka.

questions.q: What is the principal building material of the Dwarkadhish Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple is built chiefly of limestone and soft sandstone, allowing its elaborate carving.

questions.q: The flag atop the Dwarkadhish Temple is hoisted on a pole rising from the highest point of which part of the temple?
questions.explanation: The dhwaja is raised on a pole at the apex of the temple's towering shikhara, visible from far away.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the original city of Dwarka is said to have ultimately met what fate?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that the ancient city of Dwarka was swallowed by the sea after Krishna's departure.

questions.q: Within the Char Dham circuit, Dwarka is regarded as the abode marking which cardinal direction of India?
questions.explanation: In the Char Dham scheme, Dwarka represents the western dham, with Badrinath (north), Puri (east) and Rameswaram (south).

questions.q: The deity at the Dwarkadhish Temple is traditionally enshrined in an idol made of which material?
questions.explanation: The central image of Krishna at Dwarkadhish is a richly adorned idol carved from black stone.

questions.q: Dwarka is counted among the Sapta Puri, the seven sacred cities of Hinduism. Which of the following is also one of the Sapta Puri?
questions.explanation: Varanasi (Kashi) is one of the seven Sapta Puri cities, a group that also includes Dwarka, Ayodhya, Mathura, Haridwar, Kanchipuram and Ujjain.
